title: pavonia spicata
intro: Pavonia spicata, also known as Rocky Mountain Rose Mallow or Rose Mallow, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a member of the mallow family and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various skin conditions and wounds due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant's leaves, flowers, and roots have been used in herbal preparations to treat conditions such as eczema, acne, and sore throats, and to reduce fever and inflammation. In herbal preparations, Pavonia spicata is often used as a tea, tincture, or salve, and its health benefits include soothing and calming the skin, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function.
